tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh hung repeatedly on non-numeric input loops ('Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and 4.') before reaching project info prompts. Heredoc driver could not pass through the prereq/docker installer prompts. Used fallback per runbook: manually composed project skeleton from $FRAMEWORK/templates/generated/* and synthetic .claude/*.json. Note: init.sh did partially produce a project layout despite the loop, suggesting it eventually exited; treated as init-driver failure.

tests.name: framework_clone_present
tests.details: .claude/framework/ directory present (CDF clone produced by partial init.sh run)

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh not invoked because init.sh did not run cleanly; cannot assert detection ran. Recorded as gap rather than bug since the script exists at scripts/verify-install.sh.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT — heredoc driver fragile
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh <<EOF ... canned answers ... EOF — gets stuck on prereq Docker prompt (only --dry-run/--help flags exist; no --non-interactive)
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh should accept canned answers via stdin OR provide a --non-interactive/--config-file mode for automated setup and CI/UAT
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh emitted 'Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and 4.' in a tight loop on the prereq prompt and never advanced to project name. Required full manual fallback per runbook.

documentation_gaps.gap: No documented machine-readable init driver; runbook expects heredoc but init.sh's prereq prompts vary by host so canned answers are brittle. Document a stable `--non-interactive --answers <file>` mode.
No reference doc enumerating the canonical .claude/*.json shape (phase-state, process-state, build-progress, intake-progress, manifest) that fallback authors can target

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production exited 0 once .claude/intake-progress.json contained poc_mode=sponsored_poc. First attempt failed with 'Project is not in POC mode' because phase-state.json carries poc_mode but upgrade-project.sh only reads intake-progress.json and tool-preferences.json — see bug below.

tests.name: phase_state_preserved
tests.details: current_phase 2 preserved across upgrade; .project unchanged. Diff vs pre: poc_mode removed (expected for production), last_upgrade=2026-04-25 added.

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: process-state.json byte-identical post-upgrade; build_loop.history with uat-feat-1 preserved.

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Post-upgrade validation reports missing PRODUCT_MANIFESTO.md and PROJECT_BIBLE.md (expected for Phase 2), and prompts to run scripts/check-gate.sh --preflight before next Phase 1->2 transition.

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json with sentinel/reason fields and re-ran upgrade-project.sh --to-production. Script proceeded with full upgrade, did not warn or refuse, did not mention pending-approval. The pending-approval gate appears to be enforced by pre-commit-gate.sh on commits, but upgrade-project.sh itself bypasses it. Recorded as High bug below.

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh header has explicit Changelog with both BL-006 (2026-04-24, pre-commit gate blocks feat: w/o Build Loop) and BL-015 (2026-04-25, sentinel blocking + new pending-approval.sh).

framework_bugs.reproduction: In a project mid-upgrade, write {"sentinel":"pending","reason":"awaiting governance"} to .claude/pending-approval.json, then run bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production
framework_bugs.expected: Per BL-015 spirit (pending-approval blocks state-changing operations), upgrade-project.sh should refuse-or-warn when pending-approval.json exists, mirroring pre-commit-gate behavior. Runbook Section F asserts: 'if a sentinel exists, upgrade refuses or warns rather than blasting through.'
framework_bugs.actual: Upgrade ran end-to-end with no mention of pending-approval. Output contained zero matches for pending|approval|sentinel|refuse|abort related to the sentinel itself (only the routine 'APPROVAL_LOG.md exists' lines).

framework_bugs.reproduction: Project where phase-state.json has poc_mode=sponsored_poc but no .claude/intake-progress.json: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production -> '[FAIL] Project is not in POC mode'
framework_bugs.expected: upgrade-project.sh should fall back to phase-state.json when intake-progress.json is missing (init.sh writes both; recovery/manual repair scenarios may have only phase-state.json).
framework_bugs.actual: Script reads poc_mode only from intake-progress.json (lines 195-198) and tool-preferences.json. phase-state.json is ignored even though it is the canonical phase tracker for the rest of the framework.

framework_bugs.reproduction: After upgrade-project.sh --to-production, the embedded validation block prints: 'Project:  __PROJECT_NAME__' and 'Platform: __PLATFORM__ | Track: Standard | Language: __LANGUAGE__'
framework_bugs.expected: Placeholder tokens should be substituted with real project/platform/language values (already known in scope).
framework_bugs.actual: Raw __PROJECT_NAME__, __PLATFORM__, __LANGUAGE__ tokens are emitted to the terminal.

documentation_gaps.gap: upgrade-project.sh --help / header should call out that intake-progress.json is the canonical source of truth for poc_mode (or, conversely, fix the script to also accept phase-state.json)
No documentation of how upgrade-project.sh interacts with the pending-approval sentinel (BL-015) — README/CLAUDE.md should clarify whether the sentinel blocks upgrades
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/upgrade-project.sh changelog comment lines 17-21 and BL-015 spec

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: git ls-files | xargs grep -l -E for password|api_key|secret|token followed by quoted 16+ char value: 0 hits. Word matches in evaluation-prompts/* are template/doc text, not secrets.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — no FORCE/reset events occurred during this UAT; .claude/process-audit.log absent (correct behavior, append-only on bypass).

summary: Upgrade flow (sponsored_poc -> production, mcp-server, light track) preserves phase/process state and lists required artifacts, but ignores BL-015 pending-approval sentinel and reads poc_mode only from intake-progress.json (not phase-state.json); init.sh remains non-scriptable forcing manual fallback.
